result:
Done. The identical validation block (None check, type check, empty-string check) that was copy-pasted in all three parsers now lives in one place:

- **`validation.py`** (new) — `validate_input(raw)` raises `ValueError` with the same messages as before for `None`, non-string, and blank input.
- **`xml_parser.py`**, **`csv_parser.py`**, **`json_parser.py`** — each now calls `validate_input(raw)` and keeps only its parsing logic.

I verified the behavior is unchanged with a quick script: valid input parses as before, and all three error cases raise `ValueError` with the exact same messages in every parser.

One thing you may want to look at separately: all three parsers have identical *parsing* bodies too (`line.split(",")` per line), which looks like placeholder logic — the XML and JSON parsers are currently just CSV parsers by another name. I left that alone since the request was scoped to validation.
